## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  First end-to-end execution of the gauntlet suite via rbw-tP against actual fresh state. The bug class this heat exists to catch — silent first-build assumptions — surfaces here in its remaining form: case-level probes have caught what they can during walkthrough and fixture construction; BBAAX exposes the cross-section bugs only the full assembled sequence reveals.
  
  **Wrap criterion is green tally.** Not "ran to completion." Not "captured findings." Not "construction complete." This pace exists as a structural guardrail against the recurring agent failure mode where wrap is driven at "I built the thing" rather than "the thing actually works against reality." If the first run surfaces issues, fixes land here (small) or spawn follow-up paces (large) — but this pace stays open until rbw-tP runs green end-to-end.
  
  Intent-only docket. Triage decisions surface at mount time per surprise.
  
  ## Cost
  
  ~1 hour wall-clock + 2 GCP projects per attempt (one throwaway from pristine-lifecycle fixture, one canonical from canonical-establish). Re-runs after inline fixes accumulate cost; budget accordingly.
  
  ## Scope
  
  - Marshal zero per RELEASE.md §2 (signature commit on HEAD)
  - Run rbw-tP, which enforces entry contract and exec's the gauntlet suite tabtarget
  - Capture every surprise — failed assertions, unexpected diagnostics, performance anomalies, missing tabtargets, awkward UX
  - Triage routing per surprise:
    - Tiny inline (one-line bash, message clarity) — fix and re-run within this pace
    - Larger (structural, design call) — slate as follow-up pace, surface to operator before deciding
  - Record into pace closure: wall-clock total, per-phase wall-clock, cloud cost, surprises + dispositions
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract
  - **Wrap criterion is green tally** — explicit guardrail, restated to defeat drift
  - Re-runs within the pace are first-class; each re-run draws fresh quota
  - Per BBAAN's invest-fatal-on-exists and divest-deletes-production-RBRA: any retry path must respect cult-verb posture; reset between attempts goes through marshal-zero, not partial recovery
  - Per RELEASE.md failure mode contract: mid-qualification failure means start over from marshal-zero, not patch-and-continue
  
  ## Cross-pace dependencies
  
  - gauntlet-suite-assembly landed (rbw-tP and the gauntlet suite tabtarget exist)
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Restoring RBRAs — gauntlet self-mints all role credentials (BBAAN's invest cult-verb); only Payor OAuth is the operator's prerequisite
  - /rbk-prep-release integration (BBAAH)
  - Multi-day re-run scenarios
  - Cleanup beyond what rbw-tP itself does — operator peeks post-success and runs cleanup tabtargets manually if desired

27d43888  ₢BBAA7    Drop substring assertion from step 6 of hallmark-lifecycle. The Unix exit status is the canonical contract for command success/failure; producer-side display text (rbfl_rekon_hallmark's 'Hallmark not found:' message) is not normative and producers must remain free to change their display without breaking tests. Step 6 now asserts only exit_code != 0 — rekon for an abjured hallmark must die. Three failure paths collapse to two: success-when-should-die, invocation-failure. Stdout captured to step file purely for diagnostic value when debugging; the test never reads it for assertions. Rationale carried in a code comment: 'rekon's display text is not normative'. Aligns with BCG philosophy of boring-and-reliably-correct (exit-status checks) over text-scraping. Live run earlier surfaced that BUW dispatch (bud_dispatch.sh:360) merges stderr into stdout via 2>&1 — a load-bearing convention for human-readable interleaved display — so even attempting the substring check would have required selecting between two display-coupled streams. Eliminating the substring check eliminates that question. tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh clean. 24e24651  ₢BBAAX    First-run inline fix for ₢BBAAX gauntlet observability gap: emit depot project ID and Cloud Build console URL via buc_info + buc_link immediately after rbgu_http_json_lro_ok succeeds creating the depot project in rbgp_depot_levy. Operator (and agent reading the live gauntlet output stream) needs the depot's Cloud Build console URL to peek at in-flight cloud builds during the long onboarding-sequence fixture; previously the URL was only emitted per-build via the existing buc_link calls in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1021 and :1257 (after each builds.create response), leaving long quiet stretches between builds where the URL was not visible in the recent stream. Reconstructing the project ID by hand from RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX + RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER yields the wrong identifier — RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID at rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh:84 injects a 'd-' infix between the prefix and moniker (canc- + d- + canest2100004 = canc-d-canest2100004), and the agent missed this during BBAAX, sending the operator to a nonexistent project ID that produced the GCP 'You need additional access' error against canc-canest2100004 instead. Fix uses RBGC_CONSOLE_URL (already defined at rbgc_Constants.sh:78 as https://console.cloud.google.com/) suffixed with cloud-build/builds?project=${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}, matching the buc_link signature already established in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh ('Click to ', 'Open depot Cloud Build console', URL). Did not promote ZRBFC_CLOUD_QUERY_BASE from rbfc to rbgc — the constant lives in foundry-private namespace appropriately, and rbgp's URL composition pattern at depot-levy time is short enough to inline. Preceded the buc_link with a buc_info echoing RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID itself so the project ID is grep-able from the output stream even if the link rendering is consumed by terminal styling. Insertion point is between the LRO completion and the billing-link step — the project exists in GCP at this point, all subsequent depot-levy steps continue against it, so the URL is meaningful from Docket:
  ## Character
  
  First end-to-end execution of the gauntlet suite via rbw-tP against actual fresh state. The bug class this heat exists to catch — silent first-build assumptions — surfaces here in its remaining form: case-level probes have caught what they can during walkthrough and fixture construction; BBAAX exposes the cross-section bugs only the full assembled sequence reveals.
  
  **Wrap criterion is green tally.** Not "ran to completion." Not "captured findings." Not "construction complete." This pace exists as a structural guardrail against the recurring agent failure mode where wrap is driven at "I built the thing" rather than "the thing actually works against reality." If the first run surfaces issues, fixes land here (small) or spawn follow-up paces (large) — but this pace stays open until rbw-tP runs green end-to-end.
  
  Intent-only docket. Triage decisions surface at mount time per surprise.
  
  ## Cost
  
  ~1 hour wall-clock + 2 GCP projects per attempt (one throwaway from pristine-lifecycle fixture, one canonical from canonical-establish). Re-runs after inline fixes accumulate cost; budget accordingly.
  
  ## Scope
  
  - Marshal zero per RELEASE.md §2 (signature commit on HEAD)
  - Run rbw-tP, which enforces entry contract and exec's the gauntlet suite tabtarget
  - Capture every surprise — failed assertions, unexpected diagnostics, performance anomalies, missing tabtargets, awkward UX
  - Triage routing per surprise:
    - Tiny inline (one-line bash, message clarity) — fix and re-run within this pace
    - Larger (structural, design call) — slate as follow-up pace, surface to operator before deciding
  - Record into pace closure: wall-clock total, per-phase wall-clock, cloud cost, surprises + dispositions
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract
  - **Wrap criterion is green tally** — explicit guardrail, restated to defeat drift
  - Re-runs within the pace are first-class; each re-run draws fresh quota
  - Per BBAAN's invest-fatal-on-exists and divest-deletes-production-RBRA: any retry path must respect cult-verb posture; reset between attempts goes through marshal-zero, not partial recovery
  - Per RELEASE.md failure mode contract: mid-qualification failure means start over from marshal-zero, not patch-and-continue
  
  ## Cross-pace dependencies
  
  - gauntlet-suite-assembly landed (rbw-tP and the gauntlet suite tabtarget exist)
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Restoring RBRAs — gauntlet self-mints all role credentials (BBAAN's invest cult-verb); only Payor OAuth is the operator's prerequisite
  - /rbk-prep-release integration (BBAAH)
  - Multi-day re-run scenarios
  - Cleanup beyond what rbw-tP itself does — operator peeks post-success and runs cleanup tabtargets manually if desired

27d43888  ₢BBAA7    Drop substring assertion from step 6 of hallmark-lifecycle. The Unix exit status is the canonical contract for command success/failure; producer-side display text (rbfl_rekon_hallmark's 'Hallmark not found:' message) is not normative and producers must remain free to change their display without breaking tests. Step 6 now asserts only exit_code != 0 — rekon for an abjured hallmark must die. Three failure paths collapse to two: success-when-should-die, invocation-failure. Stdout captured to step file purely for diagnostic value when debugging; the test never reads it for assertions. Rationale carried in a code comment: 'rekon's display text is not normative'. Aligns with BCG philosophy of boring-and-reliably-correct (exit-status checks) over text-scraping. Live run earlier surfaced that BUW dispatch (bud_dispatch.sh:360) merges stderr into stdout via 2>&1 — a load-bearing convention for human-readable interleaved display — so even attempting the substring check would have required selecting between two display-coupled streams. Eliminating the substring check eliminates that question. tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh clean. 43704412  ₢BBAA7    Fix step 6 of hallmark-lifecycle to match the actual rekon contract for abjured hallmarks. Initial assertion expected rekon to succeed (exit 0) and report all five ark basenames as not-yes. Live run surfaced the real contract: rbfl_rekon_hallmark calls buc_die with 'Hallmark not found: <hallmark>' (rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh:616) when the hallmark's package subtree is absent from GAR, exiting non-zero. This is the cleaner contract — abjure either removes the entire registry entry or it didn't run; there's no 'partial state' for rekon to enumerate. Step 6 now positively asserts that exit-non-zero shape: invoke rekon, expect exit_code != 0 AND stderr contains 'Hallmark not found: <hallmark>'. Three failure paths surfaced explicitly (succeeded when should have died, died with wrong message, invocation failure). Combines with step 7's audit-list-restored assertion as defense-in-depth: rekon checks the package-level lookup, audit checks the registry-level enumeration, both must confirm absence. Producer string is hardcoded with a comment naming rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh as the source of truth; future drift breaks this test, which is intentional coupling. Producer side: rbfl_audit_hallmarks now calls buf_write_fact_multi inside its dedup-check block, emitting one <hallmark>.audit-hallmark file per discovered hallmark to BURD_OUTPUT_DIR / BURD_TEMP_DIR (filesystem-as-data-bus). 4df8bfbe  ₢BBAA7    Switch rbev-busybox from airgap to tether egress mode. The vessel was misconfigured as airgap with empty RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR — an inconsistent state where ordain has no source for the base image. The new airgap enshrine-preflight (added earlier in this pace) correctly fired on this and refused to ordain. Previous four-mode tests worked against the vessel only because the prior preflight silently skipped empty anchors, leaving the runtime build to fail or succeed depending on stale enshrinement state in GAR. tether mode is the correct shape for this vessel's role: a small fast cloud-built hallmark used by hallmark-lifecycle (ordain/abjure round-trip coverage) and batch-vouch (vouch state-planting). Neither consumer cares about egress mode; tether lets buildx fetch busybox:latest from Docker Hub at build time without enshrine prerequisite. ac049a40  ₢BBAA7    Dissolve four-mode fixture into hallmark-lifecycle (single load-bearing case: ordain rbev-busybox in conjure mode, audit baseline-diff to detect collateral damage, rekon all 5 ark basenames present, abjure, post-abjure rekon assert all 5 absent, final audit assert baseline restored). Replaces the 4 four-mode cases (conjure/bind/graft/kludge) which only checked abjure exit code without verifying ark removal. The unique verification value of the dissolved cases absorbs into onboarding-sequence ordain cases as verification tails (no abjure for conjure/bind since downstream per-crucible fixtures consume those hallmarks; abjure kept in graft tail since graft-demo is terminal). Kludge case drops cleanly (rbw-cKB/rbw-cKS delegate to the same rbfd_kludge machinery rbw-fk invokes, transitively covered by onboarding nameplate-kludge cases). docker-run+busybox-string assertions drop as vessel-specific noise (per-crucible fixtures cover image-runs-correctly against vessels designed for it). rbev-busybox vessel preserved (used by surviving hallmark-lifecycle case AND batch-vouch fixture); RBTDRC_FOURMODE_CONJURE_VESSEL_DIR renamed to RBTDRC_BUSYBOX_VESSEL_DIR. Onboarding tails added: ordain_conjure grows wrest+summon+inspect-3-arks+plumb_full+rekon-4-basenames+rmi (no abjure); ordain_bind grows wrest+plumb_compact+jettison-pouch+rekon-after-jettison+rmi (no abjure, surgical jettison preserves image for downstream pluml); ordain_graft grows source-layer-capture+wrest+wrested-layer-capture+DiffID-compare+rmi+abjure (terminal graft-demo, abjure safe). Helper rbtdro_ordain_capture_full added for tails needing gar_root+ark_stem facts beyond hallmark. RCG cleanup: pub(crate) on RBTDRC_FACT_GAR_ROOT/ARK_STEM/ARK_BASENAME_*/GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS and helpers rbtdrc_docker_inspect/rmi/layers_capture/rekon_basename_yes (now consumed by onboarding tails); rbtdrc_docker_run and rbtdrc_docker_pull deleted (only deleted four-mode used them); local duplicates RBTDRO_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS and RBTDRO_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE in onboarding removed in favor of rbtdrc imports. Manifest: RBTDRM_FIXTURE_FOUR_MODE renamed to RBTDRM_FIXTURE_HALLMARK_LIFECYCLE, required-colophons table entry narrowed (4 colophons: ORDAIN, ABJURE, REKON_HALLMARK, AUDIT_HALLMARKS); onboarding-sequence required-colophons grew 6→13 to cover absorbed-tail invocations. rbte_engine.sh: 'four-mode' renamed to 'hallmark-lifecycle' in 3 suite arrays (SERVICE, COMPLETE, GAUNTLET). Tabtargets: rbtd-r.FixtureRun.four-mode.sh and rbtd-s.FixtureCase.four-mode.sh renamed to .hallmark-lifecycle.sh siblings. Airgap enshrine-preflight tightening (BBAAX-exposed bug class): rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh enshrine-preflight loop now fails-fast when RBRV_EGRESS_MODE=airgap with non-empty origin and empty anchor (previously skipped silently, guaranteeing runtime build failure deeper in the pipeline). Diagnostic shape mirrors the existing 404-path remediation (buc_warn + buc_bare diagnostic + buc_tabtarget enshrine + buc_tabtarget ordain + buc_die). Tether vessels keep the permissive skip (current behavior correct — buildx fetches upstream at build time). CLAUDE.md service suite count updated (75 + 4 + 1 + 1 = 81 cases). Verified: tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh clean, tt/rbtd-t.Test.sh clean (91 unit tests), tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh clean (4 fixtures, 75 cases including handbook-render which exercises rbhocd_credential_director.sh — confirms preserved rbev-busybox handbook example reference still valid), shellcheck -S error clean on rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh, tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh ran with no tabtarget context drift (zipper enrollments unchanged). Not yet exercised live: full gauntlet (operator-initiated per pristine-tier contract; BBAAX is the verifier per cross-pace deps) and the hallmark-lifecycle fixture itself (about to run as immediate next step). Out of scope per docket: handbook prose updates (paddock framing: handbook = recipe, gauntlet = regression that runs the recipe + verifies the artifact — verification framing stays gauntlet-only); paddock update of jjp_uBuB.md lines 67/91 (operator territory). File size note per RCG: rbtdrc_crucible.rs net-decreased ~280 lines (3079 → ~2800) but remains over the 800-line guidance threshold.          
24e24651  ₢BBAAX    First-run inline fix for ₢BBAAX gauntlet observability gap: emit depot project ID and Cloud Build console URL via buc_info + buc_link immediately after rbgu_http_json_lro_ok succeeds creating the depot project in rbgp_depot_levy. Operator (and agent reading the live gauntlet output stream) needs the depot's Cloud Build console URL to peek at in-flight cloud builds during the long onboarding-sequence fixture; previously the URL was only emitted per-build via the existing buc_link calls in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1021 and :1257 (after each builds.create response), leaving long quiet stretches between builds where the URL was not visible in the recent stream. Reconstructing the project ID by hand from RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX + RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER yields the wrong identifier — RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID at rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh:84 injects a 'd-' infix between the prefix and moniker (canc- + d- + canest2100004 = canc-d-canest2100004), and the agent missed this during BBAAX, sending the operator to a nonexistent project ID that produced the GCP 'You need additional access' error against canc-canest2100004 instead. Fix uses RBGC_CONSOLE_URL (already defined at rbgc_Constants.sh:78 as https://console.cloud.google.com/) suffixed with cloud-build/builds?project=${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}, matching the buc_link signature already established in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh ('Click to ', 'Open depot Cloud Build console', URL). Did not promote ZRBFC_CLOUD_QUERY_BASE from rbfc to rbgc — the constant lives in foundry-private namespace appropriately, and rbgp's URL composition pattern at depot-levy time is short enough to inline. Preceded the buc_link with a buc_info echoing RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID itself so the project ID is grep-able from the output stream even if the link rendering is consumed by terminal styling. Insertion point is between the LRO completion and the billing-link step — the project exists in GCP at this point, all subsequent depot-levy steps continue against it, so the URL is meaningful from this moment forward. shellcheck -S error clean. Rule prescribes mechanism + one specific repair as default response shape; explicit prohibition on proliferating options or weighing consequences across alternatives; reaffirms that pace scope and segmentation are operator territory. Docket:
  ## Character
  
  First end-to-end execution of the gauntlet suite via rbw-tP against actual fresh state. The bug class this heat exists to catch — silent first-build assumptions — surfaces here in its remaining form: case-level probes have caught what they can during walkthrough and fixture construction; BBAAX exposes the cross-section bugs only the full assembled sequence reveals.
  
  **Wrap criterion is green tally.** Not "ran to completion." Not "captured findings." Not "construction complete." This pace exists as a structural guardrail against the recurring agent failure mode where wrap is driven at "I built the thing" rather than "the thing actually works against reality." If the first run surfaces issues, fixes land here (small) or spawn follow-up paces (large) — but this pace stays open until rbw-tP runs green end-to-end.
  
  Intent-only docket. Triage decisions surface at mount time per surprise.
  
  ## Cost
  
  ~1 hour wall-clock + 2 GCP projects per attempt (one throwaway from pristine-lifecycle fixture, one canonical from canonical-establish). Re-runs after inline fixes accumulate cost; budget accordingly.
  
  ## Scope
  
  - Marshal zero per RELEASE.md §2 (signature commit on HEAD)
  - Run rbw-tP, which enforces entry contract and exec's the gauntlet suite tabtarget
  - Capture every surprise — failed assertions, unexpected diagnostics, performance anomalies, missing tabtargets, awkward UX
  - Triage routing per surprise:
    - Tiny inline (one-line bash, message clarity) — fix and re-run within this pace
    - Larger (structural, design call) — slate as follow-up pace, surface to operator before deciding
  - Record into pace closure: wall-clock total, per-phase wall-clock, cloud cost, surprises + dispositions
  
  ## Constraints
  
  - Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract
  - **Wrap criterion is green tally** — explicit guardrail, restated to defeat drift
  - Re-runs within the pace are first-class; each re-run draws fresh quota
  - Per BBAAN's invest-fatal-on-exists and divest-deletes-production-RBRA: any retry path must respect cult-verb posture; reset between attempts goes through marshal-zero, not partial recovery
  - Per RELEASE.md failure mode contract: mid-qualification failure means start over from marshal-zero, not patch-and-continue
  
  ## Cross-pace dependencies
  
  - gauntlet-suite-assembly landed (rbw-tP and the gauntlet suite tabtarget exist)
  
  ## Out of scope
  
  - Restoring RBRAs — gauntlet self-mints all role credentials (BBAAN's invest cult-verb); only Payor OAuth is the operator's prerequisite
  - /rbk-prep-release integration (BBAAH)
  - Multi-day re-run scenarios
  - Cleanup beyond what rbw-tP itself does — operator peeks post-success and runs cleanup tabtargets manually if desired

27d43888  ₢BBAA7    Drop substring assertion from step 6 of hallmark-lifecycle. The Unix exit status is the canonical contract for command success/failure; producer-side display text (rbfl_rekon_hallmark's 'Hallmark not found:' message) is not normative and producers must remain free to change their display without breaking tests. Step 6 now asserts only exit_code != 0 — rekon for an abjured hallmark must die. Three failure paths collapse to two: success-when-should-die, invocation-failure. Stdout captured to step file purely for diagnostic value when debugging; the test never reads it for assertions. Rationale carried in a code comment: 'rekon's display text is not normative'. Aligns with BCG philosophy of boring-and-reliably-correct (exit-status checks) over text-scraping. Live run earlier surfaced that BUW dispatch (bud_dispatch.sh:360) merges stderr into stdout via 2>&1 — a load-bearing convention for human-readable interleaved display — so even attempting the substring check would have required selecting between two display-coupled streams. Eliminating the substring check eliminates that question. tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh clean. 43704412  ₢BBAA7    Fix step 6 of hallmark-lifecycle to match the actual rekon contract for abjured hallmarks. Initial assertion expected rekon to succeed (exit 0) and report all five ark basenames as not-yes. Live run surfaced the real contract: rbfl_rekon_hallmark calls buc_die with 'Hallmark not found: <hallmark>' (rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh:616) when the hallmark's package subtree is absent from GAR, exiting non-zero. This is the cleaner contract — abjure either removes the entire registry entry or it didn't run; there's no 'partial state' for rekon to enumerate. Step 6 now positively asserts that exit-non-zero shape: invoke rekon, expect exit_code != 0 AND stderr contains 'Hallmark not found: <hallmark>'. Three failure paths surfaced explicitly (succeeded when should have died, died with wrong message, invocation failure). Combines with step 7's audit-list-restored assertion as defense-in-depth: rekon checks the package-level lookup, audit checks the registry-level enumeration, both must confirm absence. Producer string is hardcoded with a comment naming rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh as the source of truth; future drift breaks this test, which is intentional coupling. 4df8bfbe  ₢BBAA7    Switch rbev-busybox from airgap to tether egress mode. The vessel was misconfigured as airgap with empty RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR — an inconsistent state where ordain has no source for the base image. The new airgap enshrine-preflight (added earlier in this pace) correctly fired on this and refused to ordain. Previous four-mode tests worked against the vessel only because the prior preflight silently skipped empty anchors, leaving the runtime build to fail or succeed depending on stale enshrinement state in GAR. tether mode is the correct shape for this vessel's role: a small fast cloud-built hallmark used by hallmark-lifecycle (ordain/abjure round-trip coverage) and batch-vouch (vouch state-planting). Neither consumer cares about egress mode; tether lets buildx fetch busybox:latest from Docker Hub at build time without enshrine prerequisite. Airgap-mode coverage stays in onboarding-sequence/ordain_airgap (rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap, properly enshrined via the airgap chain that lands in this pace's absorbed onboarding tails). 24e24651  ₢BBAAX    First-run inline fix for ₢BBAAX gauntlet observability gap: emit depot project ID and Cloud Build console URL via buc_info + buc_link immediately after rbgu_http_json_lro_ok succeeds creating the depot project in rbgp_depot_levy. Operator (and agent reading the live gauntlet output stream) needs the depot's Cloud Build console URL to peek at in-flight cloud builds during the long onboarding-sequence fixture; previously the URL was only emitted per-build via the existing buc_link calls in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1021 and :1257 (after each builds.create response), leaving long quiet stretches between builds where the URL was not visible in the recent stream. Reconstructing the project ID by hand from RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX + RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER yields the wrong identifier — RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID at rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh:84 injects a 'd-' infix between the prefix and moniker (canc- + d- + canest2100004 = canc-d-canest2100004), and the agent missed this during BBAAX, sending the operator to a nonexistent project ID that produced the GCP 'You need additional access' error against canc-canest2100004 instead. Fix uses RBGC_CONSOLE_URL (already defined at rbgc_Constants.sh:78 as https://console.cloud.google.com/) suffixed with cloud-build/builds?project=${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}, matching the buc_link signature already established in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh ('Click to ', 'Open depot Cloud Build console', URL). Did not promote ZRBFC_CLOUD_QUERY_BASE from rbfc to rbgc — the constant lives in foundry-private namespace appropriately, and rbgp's URL composition pattern at depot-levy time is short enough to inline. Preceded the buc_link with a buc_info echoing RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID itself so the project ID is grep-able from the output stream even if the link rendering is consumed by terminal styling. Insertion point is between the LRO completion and the billing-link step — the project exists in GCP at this point, all subsequent depot-levy steps continue against it, so the URL is meaningful from this moment forward. shellcheck -S error clean. dd5893ba  ₢BBAAX    Add Mid-execution posture rule to JJK agent context, sourced from rules-of-engagement conversation during BBAAX gauntlet first-run. Captures recurring failure mode where mid-pace surprises trigger option-proliferation responses (multi-option triage menus, scope/cost weighing, slate-vs-inline frame, follow-up-pace recommendations) that consume operator context with segmentation work that's not the agent's territory. Rule prescribes mechanism + one specific repair as default response shape; explicit prohibition on proliferating options or weighing consequences across alternatives; reaffirms that pace scope and segmentation are operator territory. Placed after Paddock posture in the agent-behavior framing cluster (Sincerity / Docket posture / Slate-time / Stale-by-mount / Docket anti-patterns / Reference discipline / Paddock posture), before Identities vs Display Names where the structural vocabulary begins. Located in jjk-claude-context.md rather than top-level CLAUDE.md because the failure mode is JJK-vocabulary-induced — outside a mount the pace-language doesn't load and the segmentation temptation doesn't surface; killing the temptation at the JJK context level is cleaner than blacklisting symptoms in a general directive. 1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Single operator prerequisite chain
10	
11	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
12	
13	## Entry contract — load-bearing
14	
15	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
16	
17	- Working tree clean
18	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
19	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
20	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
21	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
22	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
23	
24	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
25	
26	## Failure mode contract
27	
28	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
29	
30	## Release-branch execution contract
31	
32	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
33	
34	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
35	
36	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
37	
38	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
39	
40	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
41	
42	## Tier layering
43	
44	Three tiers, escalating cost:
45	
46	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
47	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
48	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
49	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
50	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
51	
52	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
53	
54	## Gauntlet test suite — design
55	
56	`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.
57	
58	**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.
59	
60	**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).
61	
62	**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:
63	
64	1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
65	2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
66	3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
67	4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — handbook-driven build walks per BBAAf)
68	5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
69	6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
70	7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: per-crucible test fixtures, vessel-exists preconditions per BBAAs)
71	
72	No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.
73	
74	## Dual purpose: handbook-regression coverage
75	
76	The gauntlet's primary purpose is release qualification (catch silent first-build assumptions). Its **secondary purpose** is automated handbook-regression coverage: ensure operator-facing onboarding handbook tracks (`rbh*`) stay in sync with working code so future operators following the prescribed steps aren't surprised by drift.
77	
78	Build-side handbook coverage lives in BBAAf (canonical-onboarding-sequence): each ordain case walks the corresponding director-mode handbook track (`rbw-Odf` conjure / `rbw-Oda` airgap / `rbw-Odb` bind / `rbw-Odg` graft) rather than calling inscribe/ordain machinery directly.
79	
80	**Build/run split — load-bearing.** Handbook walks cover the BUILD step (vessel construction) and stop when the hallmark lands in GAR. Run-side fixtures (per-crucible: tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml) stay as direct test fixtures with vessel-exists preconditions, NOT handbook walks. The split keeps run-side reusable against any vessel source — coupling them to handbook prose breaks reusability.
81	
82	Drift between fixtures and handbooks is accepted future maintenance: whoever changes one is responsible for updating the other; the gauntlet's job is to surface drift, not prevent it by construction.
83	
84	Run-side handbook coverage (`rbw-Ots`, the rack portion of `rbw-Ofc`) is explicitly out of scope for this heat.
85	
86	## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing
87	
88	Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:
89	
90	- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
91	- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.
92	
93	**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.
94	
95	Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.
96	
97	**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.
98	
99	## Suite-level fail-fast
100	
101	Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.
102	
103	## No-teardown decision
104	
105	The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:
106	
107	- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
108	- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
109	- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
110	- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.
111	
112	Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).
113	
114	## Operator scope
115	
116	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. The README.md release section remains the runbook for the gauntlet itself; the dual-purpose handbook-regression coverage protects the broader operator surface (onboarding tracks under `rbh*`) from drift.
117	
118	## Coupling to ₣A_
119	
120	Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.
121	
122	## Locked design decisions
123	
124	- **Depot identity collapse**: RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
125	- **Cult-verb naming**: SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
126	- **Build/run split**: handbook walks cover build only; per-crucible fixtures stay reusable as direct tests.
127	
128	## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh
129	
130	Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.
131	
132	**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.
133	
134	**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):
135	
136	- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
137	- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
138	- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
139	- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.
140	
141	Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.
142	
143	Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.
144	
145	## References
146	
147	- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
148	- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers
149	- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
150	- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
151	- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)
152	
153	# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAX
154	
155	## Character
156	
157	First end-to-end execution of the gauntlet suite via rbw-tP against actual fresh state. The bug class this heat exists to catch — silent first-build assumptions — surfaces here in its remaining form: case-level probes have caught what they can during walkthrough and fixture construction; BBAAX exposes the cross-section bugs only the full assembled sequence reveals.
158	
159	**Wrap criterion is green tally.** Not "ran to completion." Not "captured findings." Not "construction complete." This pace exists as a structural guardrail against the recurring agent failure mode where wrap is driven at "I built the thing" rather than "the thing actually works against reality." If the first run surfaces issues, fixes land here (small) or spawn follow-up paces (large) — but this pace stays open until rbw-tP runs green end-to-end.
160	
161	Intent-only docket. Triage decisions surface at mount time per surprise.
162	
163	## Cost
164	
165	~1 hour wall-clock + 2 GCP projects per attempt (one throwaway from pristine-lifecycle fixture, one canonical from canonical-establish). Re-runs after inline fixes accumulate cost; budget accordingly.
166	
167	## Scope
168	
169	- Marshal zero per RELEASE.md §2 (signature commit on HEAD)
170	- Run rbw-tP, which enforces entry contract and exec's the gauntlet suite tabtarget
171	- Capture every surprise — failed assertions, unexpected diagnostics, performance anomalies, missing tabtargets, awkward UX
172	- Triage routing per surprise:
173	  - Tiny inline (one-line bash, message clarity) — fix and re-run within this pace
174	  - Larger (structural, design call) — slate as follow-up pace, surface to operator before deciding
175	- Record into pace closure: wall-clock total, per-phase wall-clock, cloud cost, surprises + dispositions
176	
177	## Constraints
178	
179	- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract
180	- **Wrap criterion is green tally** — explicit guardrail, restated to defeat drift
181	- Re-runs within the pace are first-class; each re-run draws fresh quota
182	- Per BBAAN's invest-fatal-on-exists and divest-deletes-production-RBRA: any retry path must respect cult-verb posture; reset between attempts goes through marshal-zero, not partial recovery
183	- Per RELEASE.md failure mode contract: mid-qualification failure means start over from marshal-zero, not patch-and-continue
184	
185	## Cross-pace dependencies
186	
187	- gauntlet-suite-assembly landed (rbw-tP and the gauntlet suite tabtarget exist)
188	
189	## Out of scope
190	
191	- Restoring RBRAs — gauntlet self-mints all role credentials (BBAAN's invest cult-verb); only Payor OAuth is the operator's prerequisite
192	- /rbk-prep-release integration (BBAAH)
193	- Multi-day re-run scenarios
194	- Cleanup beyond what rbw-tP itself does — operator peeks post-success and runs cleanup tabtargets manually if desired
195

|----------|--------------|------|----------------|

Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.

## No-teardown decision

The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:

- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.

Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. The README.md release section remains the runbook for the gauntlet itself; the dual-purpose handbook-regression coverage protects the broader operator surface (onboarding tracks under `rbh*`) from drift.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.

## Locked design decisions

- **Depot identity collapse**: RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
- **Cult-verb naming**: SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
- **Build/run split**: handbook walks cover build only; per-crucible fixtures stay reusable as direct tests.

## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh

Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.

**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.

**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):

- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.

Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.

Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

# jjezs_pace ₢BBAAX

## Character

First end-to-end execution of the gauntlet suite via rbw-tP against actual fresh state. The bug class this heat exists to catch — silent first-build assumptions — surfaces here in its remaining form: case-level probes have caught what they can during walkthrough and fixture construction; BBAAX exposes the cross-section bugs only the full assembled sequence reveals.

**Wrap criterion is green tally.** Not "ran to completion." Not "captured findings." Not "construction complete." This pace exists as a structural guardrail against the recurring agent failure mode where wrap is driven at "I built the thing" rather than "the thing actually works against reality." If the first run surfaces issues, fixes land here (small) or spawn follow-up paces (large) — but this pace stays open until rbw-tP runs green end-to-end.

Intent-only docket. Triage decisions surface at mount time per surprise.

## Cost

~1 hour wall-clock + 2 GCP projects per attempt (one throwaway from pristine-lifecycle fixture, one canonical from canonical-establish). Re-runs after inline fixes accumulate cost; budget accordingly.

## Scope

- Marshal zero per RELEASE.md §2 (signature commit on HEAD)
- Run rbw-tP, which enforces entry contract and exec's the gauntlet suite tabtarget
- Capture every surprise — failed assertions, unexpected diagnostics, performance anomalies, missing tabtargets, awkward UX
- Triage routing per surprise:
  - Tiny inline (one-line bash, message clarity) — fix and re-run within this pace
  - Larger (structural, design call) — slate as follow-up pace, surface to operator before deciding
- Record into pace closure: wall-clock total, per-phase wall-clock, cloud cost, surprises + dispositions

## Constraints

- Honor paddock's Release-branch execution contract
- **Wrap criterion is green tally** — explicit guardrail, restated to defeat drift
- Re-runs within the pace are first-class; each re-run draws fresh quota
- Per BBAAN's invest-fatal-on-exists and divest-deletes-production-RBRA: any retry path must respect cult-verb posture; reset between attempts goes through marshal-zero, not partial recovery
- Per RELEASE.md failure mode contract: mid-qualification failure means start over from marshal-zero, not patch-and-continue

## Cross-pace dependencies

- gauntlet-suite-assembly landed (rbw-tP and the gauntlet suite tabtarget exist)

## Out of scope

- Restoring RBRAs — gauntlet self-mints all role credentials (BBAAN's invest cult-verb); only Payor OAuth is the operator's prerequisite
- /rbk-prep-release integration (BBAAH)
- Multi-day re-run scenarios
- Cleanup beyond what rbw-tP itself does — operator peeks post-success and runs cleanup tabtargets manually if desired

Absorbed verification tails into onboarding-sequence ordain_conjure (wrest+summon+inspect-3-arks+plumb_full+rekon-4-basenames+rmi), ordain_bind (wrest+plumb_compact+jettison-pouch+rekon-after+rmi), and ordain_graft (source-layer-capture+wrest+wrested-layer-capture+DiffID-compare+rmi+abjure). Added airgap-mode+empty-ANCHOR fail-fast to enshrine-preflight loop in rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh, modeled on the existing 404-path remediation diagnostic. rbev-busybox switched airgap→tether (correct shape for vessel without enshrined base). audit-hallmarks gained fact-multi emission via buf_write_fact_multi (new RBCC_fact_ext_audit_hallmark) and a Rust enumerator rbtdri_read_burv_facts_multi — stdout parsing eliminated in favor of the machine-contract path. Renamed across 3 suite arrays (SERVICE/COMPLETE/GAUNTLET) and 2 tabtargets (rbtd-r/rbtd-s.{four-mode → hallmark-lifecycle}.sh). Five debug commits captured the iteration arc (vessel config flip, fact-multi pivot, exit-status-only assertion). Out-of-scope deferred per Mid-execution posture: paddock update at jjp_uBuB.md lines 67/91 (still names 'four-mode'); full gauntlet rerun on release branch from marshal-zero (operator-initiated per pristine-tier contract — BBAAX is the verifier per cross-pace deps).
27d43888 jjb:1011-43704412:₢BBAA7:n: Drop substring assertion from step 6 of hallmark-lifecycle. The Unix exit status is the canonical contract for command success/failure; producer-side display text (rbfl_rekon_hallmark's 'Hallmark not found:' message) is not normative and producers must remain free to change their display without breaking tests. Step 6 now asserts only exit_code != 0 — rekon for an abjured hallmark must die. Three failure paths collapse to two: success-when-should-die, invocation-failure. Stdout captured to step file purely for diagnostic value when debugging; the test never reads it for assertions. Rationale carried in a code comment: 'rekon's display text is not normative'. Aligns with BCG philosophy of boring-and-reliably-correct (exit-status checks) over text-scraping. Live run earlier surfaced that BUW dispatch (bud_dispatch.sh:360) merges stderr into stdout via 2>&1 — a load-bearing convention for human-readable interleaved display — so even attempting the substring check would have required selecting between two display-coupled streams. Eliminating the substring check eliminates that question. tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh clean.

content.content:
1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# RBLM CLI - Lifecycle Marshal operations (zero, proof)
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
24	
25	######################################################################
26	# Command: zero - Zero regime to blank template for release
27	
28	rblm_zero() {
29	  buc_doc_brief "Zero regime to blank template for release qualification"
30	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
31	
32	  local -r z_rbrr="${RBBC_rbrr_file}"
33	  test -f "${z_rbrr}" || buc_die "RBRR file not found: ${z_rbrr}"
34	
35	  # Pre-checks: working tree clean and HEAD pushed.
36	  # Marshal-zero auto-commits its mutations; both invariants must hold to keep
37	  # the resulting commit purely the marshal-zero state.
38	  mkdir -p "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}" || buc_die "Failed to create temp directory"
39	  local -r z_status_temp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rblm_zero_status.txt"
40	  local -r z_upstream_temp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rblm_zero_upstream.txt"
41	  local -r z_unpushed_temp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rblm_zero_unpushed.txt"
42	
43	  git status --porcelain > "${z_status_temp}" || buc_die "git status failed"
44	  test ! -s "${z_status_temp}" || buc_die "Working tree not clean — commit or discard before marshal-zero. See: ${z_status_temp}"
45	
46	  if ! git rev-parse --abbrev-ref --symbolic-full-name '@{u}' > "${z_upstream_temp}" 2>&1; then
47	    buc_die "Current branch has no upstream — set tracking and push before marshal-zero. See: ${z_upstream_temp}"
48	  fi
49	
50	  git rev-list '@{u}..HEAD' > "${z_unpushed_temp}" || buc_die "git rev-list failed"
51	  test ! -s "${z_unpushed_temp}" || buc_die "HEAD has unpushed commits — push before marshal-zero. See: ${z_unpushed_temp}"
52	
53	  # Discover secrets dir and vessel dir for pre-confirmation inventory
54	  local z_secrets_dir=""
55	  local z_vessel_dir=""
56	  local z_secrets_line=""
57	  while IFS= read -r z_secrets_line || test -n "${z_secrets_line}"; do
58	    case "${z_secrets_line}" in
59	      RBRR_SECRETS_DIR=*) z_secrets_dir="${z_secrets_line#RBRR_SECRETS_DIR=}" ;;
60	      RBRR_VESSEL_DIR=*)  z_vessel_dir="${z_secrets_line#RBRR_VESSEL_DIR=}"  ;;
61	    esac
62	  done < "${z_rbrr}"
63	
64	  buh_section "Marshal Zero"
65	  buh_line "  Target: ${z_rbrr}"
66	  buh_e
67	  buh_line "  RBRR fields blanked (zeroed to onboarding start):"
68	  buh_line "    RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX,"
69	  buh_line "    RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER"
70	  buh_e
71	  buh_line "  RBRR fields pre-filled to defaults:"
72	  buh_line "    RBRR_DNS_SERVER, RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE, RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT,"
73	  buh_line "    RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS, RBRR_GCP_REGION,"
74	  buh_line "    RBRR_VESSEL_DIR, RBRR_SECRETS_DIR"
75	  buh_e
76	  buh_line "  Depot credentials DELETED (tied to prior depot):"
77	  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}"; then
78	    local z_preview=""
79	    local z_any_cred=0
80	    for z_preview in "${RBCC_role_governor}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_director}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_retriever}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_assay}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"; do
81	      if test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${z_preview}"; then
82	        buh_line "    ${z_secrets_dir}/${z_preview}"
83	        z_any_cred=1
84	      fi
85	    done
86	    test "${z_any_cred}" = "1" || buh_line "    (none present)"
87	  else
88	    buh_line "    (secrets dir not configured)"
89	  fi
90	  buh_e
91	  buh_line "  Vessel hallmarks BLANKED (stale after depot change):"
92	  local z_np_preview=""
93	  local z_any_np=0
94	  for z_np_preview in "${RBBC_dot_dir}"/*/rbrn.env; do
95	    test -f "${z_np_preview}" || continue
96	    buh_line "    ${z_np_preview}"
97	    z_any_np=1
98	  done
99	  test "${z_any_np}" = "1" || buh_line "    (no nameplates found)"
100	  buh_e
101	  buh_line "  Vessel regime fields BLANKED (depot-scoped, stale after depot change):"
102	  buh_line "    RBRV_RELIQUARY, RBRV_IMAGE_*_ANCHOR in all rbrv.env"
103	  if test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" && test -d "${z_vessel_dir}"; then
104	    local z_vr_preview=""
105	    local z_any_vr=0
106	    for z_vr_preview in "${z_vessel_dir}"/*/rbrv.env; do
107	      test -f "${z_vr_preview}" || continue
108	      buh_line "    ${z_vr_preview}"
109	      z_any_vr=1
110	    done
111	    test "${z_any_vr}" = "1" || buh_line "    (no vessel regimes found)"
112	  else
113	    buh_line "    (vessel dir not configured or missing)"
114	  fi
115	  buh_e
116	  buh_line "  Preserved (payor-scoped, survives depot change):"
117	  buh_line "    ${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_role_payor}/${RBCC_rbro_file}"
118	  buh_e
119	  buh_line "  On completion, marshal-zero auto-commits the in-tree mutations."
120	  buh_e
121	  buc_require "Proceed with marshal zero?" "zero"
122	
123	  local -r z_tmp="${z_rbrr}.tmp"
124	  local z_line=""
125	  while IFS= read -r z_line; do
126	    case "${z_line}" in
127	      # Pre-selected defaults
128	      RBRR_DNS_SERVER=*)                    printf '%s\n' "RBRR_DNS_SERVER=8.8.8.8"                     ;;
129	      RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE=*)              printf '%s\n' "RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE=e2-standard-2"         ;;
130	      RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT=*)                   printf '%s\n' "RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT=2700s"                      ;;
131	      RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS=*)     printf '%s\n' "RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS=3"            ;;
132	      RBRR_GCP_REGION=*)                    printf '%s\n' "RBRR_GCP_REGION=us-central1"                 ;;
133	      RBRR_VESSEL_DIR=*)                    printf '%s\n' "RBRR_VESSEL_DIR=rbev-vessels"                ;;
134	      RBRR_SECRETS_DIR=*)                   printf '%s\n' "RBRR_SECRETS_DIR=../station-files/secrets"   ;;
135	      # Site-specific fields blanked
136	      RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX=*)                  printf '%s\n' "RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX="                          ;;
137	      RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX=*)                printf '%s\n' "RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX="                        ;;
138	      RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER=*)                 printf '%s\n' "RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER="                         ;;
139	      RBRR_GCB_WORKER_POOL=*)               continue                                                      ;;
140	      # Everything else passes through (comments, shebang, blanks)
141	      *)                                    printf '%s\n' "${z_line}"                                   ;;
142	    esac
143	  done < "${z_rbrr}" > "${z_tmp}" && mv "${z_tmp}" "${z_rbrr}"
144	
145	  # Remove depot-scoped RBRA files (governor, director, retriever).
146	  # z_secrets_dir already extracted above for pre-confirmation inventory.
147	  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}"; then
148	    local z_rbra=""
149	    for z_rbra in "${RBCC_role_governor}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_director}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_retriever}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_assay}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"; do
150	      if test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${z_rbra}"; then
151	        rm "${z_secrets_dir}/${z_rbra}" || buc_die "Failed to remove: ${z_secrets_dir}/${z_rbra}"
152	        buh_line "  Removed stale depot credential: ${z_rbra}"
153	      fi
154	    done
155	  fi
156	
157	  # Blank hallmark values in all vessel nameplates.
158	  # Hallmarks reference images built against the prior depot — they
159	  # become stale after reset.  Blanking them causes the onboarding guide
160	  # to require conjure & vouch before declaring setup complete.
161	  local z_np=""
162	  local z_np_tmp=""
163	  for z_np in "${RBBC_dot_dir}"/*/rbrn.env; do
164	    test -f "${z_np}" || continue
165	    z_np_tmp="${z_np}.tmp"
166	    while IFS= read -r z_line; do
167	      case "${z_line}" in
168	        RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK=*)  printf '%s\n' "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK=" ;;
169	        RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK=*)  printf '%s\n' "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK=" ;;
170	        *)                           printf '%s\n' "${z_line}"                  ;;
171	      esac
172	    done < "${z_np}" > "${z_np_tmp}" && mv "${z_np_tmp}" "${z_np}"
173	    buh_line "  Blanked hallmarks: ${z_np}"
174	  done
175	
176	  # Blank depot-scoped fields in all vessel regime files.
177	  # RBRV_RELIQUARY references a reliquary inscribed to the prior depot's GAR.
178	  # RBRV_IMAGE_*_ANCHOR references enshrined base images in the prior depot's GAR.
179	  # Both become stale after depot change — onboarding requires inscribe + enshrine.
180	  if test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" && test -d "${z_vessel_dir}"; then
181	    local z_vr=""
182	    local z_vr_tmp=""
183	    for z_vr in "${z_vessel_dir}"/*/rbrv.env; do
184	      test -f "${z_vr}" || continue
185	      z_vr_tmp="${z_vr}.tmp"
186	      while IFS= read -r z_line; do
187	        case "${z_line}" in
188	          RBRV_RELIQUARY=*)       printf '%s\n' "RBRV_RELIQUARY="       ;;
189	          RBRV_IMAGE_*_ANCHOR=*)  printf '%s\n' "${z_line%%=*}="        ;;
190	          *)                      printf '%s\n' "${z_line}"             ;;
191	        esac
192	      done < "${z_vr}" > "${z_vr_tmp}" && mv "${z_vr_tmp}" "${z_vr}"
193	      buh_line "  Blanked depot-scoped fields: ${z_vr}"
194	    done
195	  fi
196	
197	  buh_line "  Zero complete: ${z_rbrr}"
198	  buh_e
199	
200	  # Auto-commit the in-tree mutations so post-zero state is captured as a single
201	  # commit. RBRA deletions (outside repo) are not staged.
202	  buc_step "Committing marshal-zero state"
203	  git add "${z_rbrr}" || buc_die "Failed to stage RBRR file"
204	
205	  local z_stage=""
206	  for z_stage in "${RBBC_dot_dir}"/*/rbrn.env; do
207	    test -f "${z_stage}" || continue
208	    git add "${z_stage}" || buc_die "Failed to stage: ${z_stage}"
209	  done
210	  if test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" && test -d "${z_vessel_dir}"; then
211	    for z_stage in "${z_vessel_dir}"/*/rbrv.env; do
212	      test -f "${z_stage}" || continue
213	      git add "${z_stage}" || buc_die "Failed to stage: ${z_stage}"
214	    done
215	  fi
216	
217	  if git diff --cached --quiet; then
218	    buh_line "  No changes to commit — already in marshal-zero state"
219	  else
220	    git commit -m "Marshal Zero — release qualification reset" || buc_die "Marshal-zero commit failed"
221	    buh_line "  Marshal-zero state committed"
222	  fi
223	  buh_e
224	
225	  buh_line "  Next: verify onboarding guide detects blank state:"
226	  buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
227	  buc_success "Regime zeroed to blank template"
228	}
229	
230	######################################################################
231	# Command: proof - Create isolated clone for release ceremony testing
232	
233	rblm_proof() {
234	  buc_doc_brief "Create proof copy of repository for release ceremony testing"
235	  buc_doc_param "target_dir" "Absolute path to target directory (must not exist)"
236	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
237	
238	  local z_target_dir="${1:-}"
239	  test -n "${z_target_dir}" || buc_die "Target directory path is required"
240	
241	  # Validate absolute path
242	  case "${z_target_dir}" in
243	    /*) ;;
244	    *)  buc_die "Target directory must be an absolute path: ${z_target_dir}" ;;
245	  esac
246	
247	  # Must not exist
248	  test ! -e "${z_target_dir}" || buc_die "Target directory already exists: ${z_target_dir}"
249	
250	  # Get origin URL via temp file (BCG: temp file instead of command substitution)
251	  mkdir -p "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}" || buc_die "Failed to create temp directory"
252	  local -r z_origin_temp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rblm_origin_url.txt"
253	  git remote get-url origin > "${z_origin_temp}" || buc_die "Failed to get origin URL"
254	  local z_origin_url=$(<"${z_origin_temp}")
255	  test -n "${z_origin_url}" || buc_die "Origin URL is empty"
256	
257	  # Derive repo name from origin URL (BCG: parameter expansion, not basename)
258	  local z_repo_name="${z_origin_url##*/}"
259	  z_repo_name="${z_repo_name%.git}"
260	  test -n "${z_repo_name}" || buc_die "Could not derive repo name from origin: ${z_origin_url}"
261	
262	  # Resolve source station-files directory from BURD_STATION_FILE
263	  # BURD_STATION_FILE is absolute (set by launcher from BURC_STATION_FILE)
264	  local -r z_station_file_dir="${BURD_STATION_FILE%/*}"
265	  local -r z_source_station_dir="${z_station_file_dir}"
266	  local -r z_source_secrets="${z_station_file_dir}/secrets"
267	
268	  # Compute target paths
269	  local -r z_clone_dir="${z_target_dir}/${z_repo_name}"
270	  local -r z_target_station_dir="${z_target_dir}/station-files"
271	  local -r z_target_secrets="${z_target_station_dir}/secrets"
272	
273	  # Get OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM URL if configured
274	  local z_upstream_url=""
275	  local -r z_upstream_temp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rblm_upstream_url.txt"
276	  if git remote get-url OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM > "${z_upstream_temp}" 2>/dev/null; then
277	    z_upstream_url=$(<"${z_upstream_temp}")
278	  fi
279	
280	  # Present plan
281	  buh_section "Marshal Proof"
282	  buh_line "  Target directory:     ${z_target_dir}"
283	  buh_line "  Clone subdirectory:   ${z_clone_dir}"
284	  buh_line "  Station files:        ${z_target_station_dir}"
285	  buh_line "  Repo name:            ${z_repo_name}"
286	  buh_e
287	  buh_line "  Source station dir:   ${z_source_station_dir}"
288	  buh_line "  Source secrets:       ${z_source_secrets}"
289	  buh_e
290	  buh_line "  Clone origin:         ${z_origin_url}"
291	  if test -n "${z_upstream_url}"; then
292	    buh_line "  OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM: ${z_upstream_url}"
293	  else
294	    buh_line "  OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM: (not configured)"
295	  fi
296	  buh_e
297	
298	  # Create target directory
299	  buc_step "Creating target directory"
300	  mkdir "${z_target_dir}" || buc_die "Failed to create target directory: ${z_target_dir}"
301	
302	  # Clone repository
303	  buc_step "Cloning repository to ${z_clone_dir}"
304	  git clone . "${z_clone_dir}" || buc_die "Failed to clone repository"
305	
306	  # Set origin in clone to real origin (not local path from git clone .)
307	  buc_step "Configuring remotes in clone"
308	  git -C "${z_clone_dir}" remote set-url origin "${z_origin_url}" || buc_die "Failed to set origin URL in clone"
309	
310	  # Set OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM if configured on source
311	  if test -n "${z_upstream_url}"; then
312	    if git -C "${z_clone_dir}" remote get-url OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM >/dev/null 2>&1; then
313	      git -C "${z_clone_dir}" remote set-url OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM "${z_upstream_url}" || buc_die "Failed to set OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM in clone"
314	    else
315	      git -C "${z_clone_dir}" remote add OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM "${z_upstream_url}" || buc_die "Failed to add OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM to clone"
316	    fi
317	  fi
318	
319	  # Copy station files (all .env files from source station directory)
320	  buc_step "Copying station files"
321	  mkdir -p "${z_target_secrets}" || buc_die "Failed to create secrets directory"
322	
323	  local z_env_file=""
324	  local z_env_name=""
325	  for z_env_file in "${z_source_station_dir}"/*.env; do
326	    test -f "${z_env_file}" || continue
327	    z_env_name="${z_env_file##*/}"
328	    cp "${z_env_file}" "${z_target_station_dir}/${z_env_name}" || buc_die "Failed to copy: ${z_env_name}"
329	    buh_line "  Copied: ${z_env_name}"
330	  done
331	
332	  # Copy secrets (credential files)
333	  if test -d "${z_source_secrets}"; then
334	    local z_cred=""
335	    local z_cred_name=""
336	    local z_any_copied=0
337	    for z_cred in "${z_source_secrets}"/*.env; do
338	      test -f "${z_cred}" || continue
339	      z_cred_name="${z_cred##*/}"
340	      cp "${z_cred}" "${z_target_secrets}/${z_cred_name}" || buc_die "Failed to copy credential: ${z_cred_name}"
341	      chmod 600 "${z_target_secrets}/${z_cred_name}" || buc_die "Failed to set permissions on: ${z_cred_name}"
342	      buh_line "  Copied: secrets/${z_cred_name}"
343	      z_any_copied=1
344	    done
345	    test "${z_any_copied}" = "1" || buh_line "  No credential files found in: ${z_source_secrets}"
346	  else
347	    buh_line "  Warning: source secrets directory not found: ${z_source_secrets}"
348	  fi
349	
350	  buh_e
351	  buh_line "  Proof complete: ${z_clone_dir}"
352	  buh_e
353	  buh_line "  To use the duplicate, start Claude Code from:"
354	  buh_line "    ${z_clone_dir}"
355	  buc_success "Proof copy created at ${z_target_dir}"
356	}
357	
358	######################################################################
359	# Command: generate - Generate tabtarget context markdown from zipper registry
360	
361	rblm_generate() {
362	  buc_doc_brief "Generate tabtarget context markdown from zipper registry"
363	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
364	
365	  local -r z_target="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}/rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md"
366	  local -r z_tmp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md"
367	
368	  buz_emit_context "${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}" > "${z_tmp}" || buc_die "Failed to generate context"
369	
370	  if test -f "${z_target}" && diff -q "${z_tmp}" "${z_target}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
371	    buc_log_args "Context file already up to date: ${z_target}"
372	  else
373	    cp "${z_tmp}" "${z_target}" || buc_die "Failed to write: ${z_target}"
374	    buc_log_args "Generated: ${z_target}"
375	  fi
376	
377	  buc_success "Context generation complete"
378	}
379	
380	######################################################################
381	# Furnish and Main
382	
383	zrblm_furnish() {
384	  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
385	  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
386	  buc_doc_env "BURD_TEMP_DIR         " "Temporary directory for this invocation (dispatch-provided)"
387	  buc_doc_env "BURD_TABTARGET_DIR    " "Tabtarget directory (dispatch-provided)"
388	  buc_doc_env_done || return 0
389	
390	  source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh"
391	  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"
392	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh" || buc_die "Failed to source rbcc_Constants.sh"
393	
394	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"         || buc_die "Failed to source buym_yelp.sh"
395	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buh_handbook.sh"      || buc_die "Failed to source buh_handbook.sh"
396	  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"     || buc_die "Failed to source buz_zipper.sh"
397	  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbz_zipper.sh"    || buc_die "Failed to source rbz_zipper.sh"
398	  zbuz_kindle
399	  zrbz_kindle
400	}
401	
402	buc_execute rblm_ "Lifecycle Marshal" zrblm_furnish "$@"
403	
404	# eof
405
