#
# RBLM CLI - Lifecycle Marshal operations (zero, proof)

set -euo pipefail

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"

######################################################################
# Command: zero - Zero regime to blank template for release

rblm_zero() {
  buc_doc_brief "Zero regime to blank template for release qualification"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local -r z_rbrr="${RBBC_rbrr_file}"
  test -f "${z_rbrr}" || buc_die "RBRR file not found: ${z_rbrr}"

  # Pre-checks: working tree clean and HEAD pushed.
  # Marshal-zero auto-commits its mutations; both invariants must hold to keep
  # the resulting commit purely the marshal-zero state.
  mkdir -p "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}" || buc_die "Failed to create temp directory"
  local -r z_status_temp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rblm_zero_status.txt"
  local -r z_upstream_temp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rblm_zero_upstream.txt"
  local -r z_unpushed_temp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rblm_zero_unpushed.txt"

  git status --porcelain > "${z_status_temp}" || buc_die "git status failed"
  test ! -s "${z_status_temp}" || buc_die "Working tree not clean — commit or discard before marshal-zero. See: ${z_status_temp}"

  if ! git rev-parse --abbrev-ref --symbolic-full-name '@{u}' > "${z_upstream_temp}" 2>&1; then
    buc_die "Current branch has no upstream — set tracking and push before marshal-zero. See: ${z_upstream_temp}"
  fi

  git rev-list '@{u}..HEAD' > "${z_unpushed_temp}" || buc_die "git rev-list failed"
  test ! -s "${z_unpushed_temp}" || buc_die "HEAD has unpushed commits — push before marshal-zero. See: ${z_unpushed_temp}"

  # Discover secrets dir and vessel dir for pre-confirmation inventory
  local z_secrets_dir=""
  local z_vessel_dir=""
  local z_secrets_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_secrets_line || test -n "${z_secrets_line}"; do
    case "${z_secrets_line}" in
      RBRR_SECRETS_DIR=*) z_secrets_dir="${z_secrets_line#RBRR_SECRETS_DIR=}" ;;
      RBRR_VESSEL_DIR=*)  z_vessel_dir="${z_secrets_line#RBRR_VESSEL_DIR=}"  ;;
    esac
  done < "${z_rbrr}"

  buh_section "Marshal Zero"
  buh_line "  Target: ${z_rbrr}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  RBRR fields blanked (zeroed to onboarding start):"
  buh_line "    RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX, RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX,"
  buh_line "    RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  RBRR fields pre-filled to defaults:"
  buh_line "    RBRR_DNS_SERVER, RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE, RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT,"
  buh_line "    RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS, RBRR_GCP_REGION,"
  buh_line "    RBRR_VESSEL_DIR, RBRR_SECRETS_DIR"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  Depot credentials DELETED (tied to prior depot):"
  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}"; then
    local z_preview=""
    local z_any_cred=0
    for z_preview in "${RBCC_role_governor}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_director}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_retriever}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_assay}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"; do
      if test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${z_preview}"; then
        buh_line "    ${z_secrets_dir}/${z_preview}"
        z_any_cred=1
      fi
    done
    test "${z_any_cred}" = "1" || buh_line "    (none present)"
  else
    buh_line "    (secrets dir not configured)"
  fi
  buh_e
  buh_line "  Vessel hallmarks BLANKED (stale after depot change):"
  local z_np_preview=""
  local z_any_np=0
  for z_np_preview in "${RBBC_dot_dir}"/*/rbrn.env; do
    test -f "${z_np_preview}" || continue
    buh_line "    ${z_np_preview}"
    z_any_np=1
  done
  test "${z_any_np}" = "1" || buh_line "    (no nameplates found)"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  Vessel regime fields BLANKED (depot-scoped, stale after depot change):"
  buh_line "    RBRV_RELIQUARY, RBRV_IMAGE_*_ANCHOR in all rbrv.env"
  if test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" && test -d "${z_vessel_dir}"; then
    local z_vr_preview=""
    local z_any_vr=0
    for z_vr_preview in "${z_vessel_dir}"/*/rbrv.env; do
      test -f "${z_vr_preview}" || continue
      buh_line "    ${z_vr_preview}"
      z_any_vr=1
    done
    test "${z_any_vr}" = "1" || buh_line "    (no vessel regimes found)"
  else
    buh_line "    (vessel dir not configured or missing)"
  fi
  buh_e
  buh_line "  Preserved (payor-scoped, survives depot change):"
  buh_line "    ${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_role_payor}/${RBCC_rbro_file}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  On completion, marshal-zero auto-commits the in-tree mutations."
  buh_e
  buc_require "Proceed with marshal zero?" "zero"

  local -r z_tmp="${z_rbrr}.tmp"
  local z_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_line; do
    case "${z_line}" in
      # Pre-selected defaults
      RBRR_DNS_SERVER=*)                    printf '%s\n' "RBRR_DNS_SERVER=8.8.8.8"                     ;;
      RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE=*)              printf '%s\n' "RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE=e2-standard-2"         ;;
      RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT=*)                   printf '%s\n' "RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT=2700s"                      ;;
      RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS=*)     printf '%s\n' "RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS=3"            ;;
      RBRR_GCP_REGION=*)                    printf '%s\n' "RBRR_GCP_REGION=us-central1"                 ;;
      RBRR_VESSEL_DIR=*)                    printf '%s\n' "RBRR_VESSEL_DIR=rbev-vessels"                ;;
      RBRR_SECRETS_DIR=*)                   printf '%s\n' "RBRR_SECRETS_DIR=../station-files/secrets"   ;;
      # Site-specific fields blanked
      RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX=*)                  printf '%s\n' "RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX="                          ;;
      RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX=*)                printf '%s\n' "RBRR_RUNTIME_PREFIX="                        ;;
      RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER=*)                 printf '%s\n' "RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER="                         ;;
      RBRR_GCB_WORKER_POOL=*)               continue                                                      ;;
      # Everything else passes through (comments, shebang, blanks)
      *)                                    printf '%s\n' "${z_line}"                                   ;;
    esac
  done < "${z_rbrr}" > "${z_tmp}" && mv "${z_tmp}" "${z_rbrr}"

  # Remove depot-scoped RBRA files (governor, director, retriever).
  # z_secrets_dir already extracted above for pre-confirmation inventory.
  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}"; then
    local z_rbra=""
    for z_rbra in "${RBCC_role_governor}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_director}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_retriever}/${RBCC_rbra_file}" "${RBCC_role_assay}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"; do
      if test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${z_rbra}"; then
        rm "${z_secrets_dir}/${z_rbra}" || buc_die "Failed to remove: ${z_secrets_dir}/${z_rbra}"
        buh_line "  Removed stale depot credential: ${z_rbra}"
      fi
    done
  fi

  # Blank hallmark values in all vessel nameplates.
  # Hallmarks reference images built against the prior depot — they
  # become stale after reset.  Blanking them causes the onboarding guide
  # to require conjure & vouch before declaring setup complete.
  local z_np=""
  local z_np_tmp=""
  for z_np in "${RBBC_dot_dir}"/*/rbrn.env; do
    test -f "${z_np}" || continue
    z_np_tmp="${z_np}.tmp"
    while IFS= read -r z_line; do
      case "${z_line}" in
        RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK=*)  printf '%s\n' "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK=" ;;
        RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK=*)  printf '%s\n' "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK=" ;;
        *)                           printf '%s\n' "${z_line}"                  ;;
      esac
    done < "${z_np}" > "${z_np_tmp}" && mv "${z_np_tmp}" "${z_np}"
    buh_line "  Blanked hallmarks: ${z_np}"
  done

  # Blank depot-scoped fields in all vessel regime files.
  # RBRV_RELIQUARY references a reliquary inscribed to the prior depot's GAR.
  # RBRV_IMAGE_*_ANCHOR references enshrined base images in the prior depot's GAR.
  # Both become stale after depot change — onboarding requires inscribe + enshrine.
  if test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" && test -d "${z_vessel_dir}"; then
    local z_vr=""
    local z_vr_tmp=""
    for z_vr in "${z_vessel_dir}"/*/rbrv.env; do
      test -f "${z_vr}" || continue
      z_vr_tmp="${z_vr}.tmp"
      while IFS= read -r z_line; do
        case "${z_line}" in
          RBRV_RELIQUARY=*)       printf '%s\n' "RBRV_RELIQUARY="       ;;
          RBRV_IMAGE_*_ANCHOR=*)  printf '%s\n' "${z_line%%=*}="        ;;
          *)                      printf '%s\n' "${z_line}"             ;;
        esac
      done < "${z_vr}" > "${z_vr_tmp}" && mv "${z_vr_tmp}" "${z_vr}"
      buh_line "  Blanked depot-scoped fields: ${z_vr}"
    done
  fi

  buh_line "  Zero complete: ${z_rbrr}"
  buh_e

  # Auto-commit the in-tree mutations so post-zero state is captured as a single
  # commit. RBRA deletions (outside repo) are not staged.
  buc_step "Committing marshal-zero state"
  git add "${z_rbrr}" || buc_die "Failed to stage RBRR file"

  local z_stage=""
  for z_stage in "${RBBC_dot_dir}"/*/rbrn.env; do
    test -f "${z_stage}" || continue
    git add "${z_stage}" || buc_die "Failed to stage: ${z_stage}"
  done
  if test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" && test -d "${z_vessel_dir}"; then
    for z_stage in "${z_vessel_dir}"/*/rbrv.env; do
      test -f "${z_stage}" || continue
      git add "${z_stage}" || buc_die "Failed to stage: ${z_stage}"
    done
  fi

  if git diff --cached --quiet; then
    buh_line "  No changes to commit — already in marshal-zero state"
  else
    git commit -m "Marshal Zero — release qualification reset" || buc_die "Marshal-zero commit failed"
    buh_line "  Marshal-zero state committed"
  fi
  buh_e

  buh_line "  Next: verify onboarding guide detects blank state:"
  buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
  buc_success "Regime zeroed to blank template"
}

######################################################################
# Command: proof - Create isolated clone for release ceremony testing

rblm_proof() {
  buc_doc_brief "Create proof copy of repository for release ceremony testing"
  buc_doc_param "target_dir" "Absolute path to target directory (must not exist)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local z_target_dir="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_target_dir}" || buc_die "Target directory path is required"

  # Validate absolute path
  case "${z_target_dir}" in
    /*) ;;
    *)  buc_die "Target directory must be an absolute path: ${z_target_dir}" ;;
  esac

  # Must not exist
  test ! -e "${z_target_dir}" || buc_die "Target directory already exists: ${z_target_dir}"

  # Get origin URL via temp file (BCG: temp file instead of command substitution)
  mkdir -p "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}" || buc_die "Failed to create temp directory"
  local -r z_origin_temp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rblm_origin_url.txt"
  git remote get-url origin > "${z_origin_temp}" || buc_die "Failed to get origin URL"
  local z_origin_url=$(<"${z_origin_temp}")
  test -n "${z_origin_url}" || buc_die "Origin URL is empty"

  # Derive repo name from origin URL (BCG: parameter expansion, not basename)
  local z_repo_name="${z_origin_url##*/}"
  z_repo_name="${z_repo_name%.git}"
  test -n "${z_repo_name}" || buc_die "Could not derive repo name from origin: ${z_origin_url}"

  # Resolve source station-files directory from BURD_STATION_FILE
  # BURD_STATION_FILE is absolute (set by launcher from BURC_STATION_FILE)
  local -r z_station_file_dir="${BURD_STATION_FILE%/*}"
  local -r z_source_station_dir="${z_station_file_dir}"
  local -r z_source_secrets="${z_station_file_dir}/secrets"

  # Compute target paths
  local -r z_clone_dir="${z_target_dir}/${z_repo_name}"
  local -r z_target_station_dir="${z_target_dir}/station-files"
  local -r z_target_secrets="${z_target_station_dir}/secrets"

  # Get OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM URL if configured
  local z_upstream_url=""
  local -r z_upstream_temp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rblm_upstream_url.txt"
  if git remote get-url OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM > "${z_upstream_temp}" 2>/dev/null; then
    z_upstream_url=$(<"${z_upstream_temp}")
  fi

  # Present plan
  buh_section "Marshal Proof"
  buh_line "  Target directory:     ${z_target_dir}"
  buh_line "  Clone subdirectory:   ${z_clone_dir}"
  buh_line "  Station files:        ${z_target_station_dir}"
  buh_line "  Repo name:            ${z_repo_name}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  Source station dir:   ${z_source_station_dir}"
  buh_line "  Source secrets:       ${z_source_secrets}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  Clone origin:         ${z_origin_url}"
  if test -n "${z_upstream_url}"; then
    buh_line "  OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM: ${z_upstream_url}"
  else
    buh_line "  OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM: (not configured)"
  fi
  buh_e

  # Create target directory
  buc_step "Creating target directory"
  mkdir "${z_target_dir}" || buc_die "Failed to create target directory: ${z_target_dir}"

  # Clone repository
  buc_step "Cloning repository to ${z_clone_dir}"
  git clone . "${z_clone_dir}" || buc_die "Failed to clone repository"

  # Set origin in clone to real origin (not local path from git clone .)
  buc_step "Configuring remotes in clone"
  git -C "${z_clone_dir}" remote set-url origin "${z_origin_url}" || buc_die "Failed to set origin URL in clone"

  # Set OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM if configured on source
  if test -n "${z_upstream_url}"; then
    if git -C "${z_clone_dir}" remote get-url OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      git -C "${z_clone_dir}" remote set-url OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM "${z_upstream_url}" || buc_die "Failed to set OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM in clone"
    else
      git -C "${z_clone_dir}" remote add OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM "${z_upstream_url}" || buc_die "Failed to add OPEN_SOURCE_UPSTREAM to clone"
    fi
  fi

  # Copy station files (all .env files from source station directory)
  buc_step "Copying station files"
  mkdir -p "${z_target_secrets}" || buc_die "Failed to create secrets directory"

  local z_env_file=""
  local z_env_name=""
  for z_env_file in "${z_source_station_dir}"/*.env; do
    test -f "${z_env_file}" || continue
    z_env_name="${z_env_file##*/}"
    cp "${z_env_file}" "${z_target_station_dir}/${z_env_name}" || buc_die "Failed to copy: ${z_env_name}"
    buh_line "  Copied: ${z_env_name}"
  done

  # Copy secrets (credential files)
  if test -d "${z_source_secrets}"; then
    local z_cred=""
    local z_cred_name=""
    local z_any_copied=0
    for z_cred in "${z_source_secrets}"/*.env; do
      test -f "${z_cred}" || continue
      z_cred_name="${z_cred##*/}"
      cp "${z_cred}" "${z_target_secrets}/${z_cred_name}" || buc_die "Failed to copy credential: ${z_cred_name}"
      chmod 600 "${z_target_secrets}/${z_cred_name}" || buc_die "Failed to set permissions on: ${z_cred_name}"
      buh_line "  Copied: secrets/${z_cred_name}"
      z_any_copied=1
    done
    test "${z_any_copied}" = "1" || buh_line "  No credential files found in: ${z_source_secrets}"
  else
    buh_line "  Warning: source secrets directory not found: ${z_source_secrets}"
  fi

  buh_e
  buh_line "  Proof complete: ${z_clone_dir}"
  buh_e
  buh_line "  To use the duplicate, start Claude Code from:"
  buh_line "    ${z_clone_dir}"
  buc_success "Proof copy created at ${z_target_dir}"
}

######################################################################
# Command: generate - Generate tabtarget context markdown from zipper registry

rblm_generate() {
  buc_doc_brief "Generate tabtarget context markdown from zipper registry"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  local -r z_target="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}/rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md"
  local -r z_tmp="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md"

  buz_emit_context "${BURD_TABTARGET_DIR}" > "${z_tmp}" || buc_die "Failed to generate context"

  if test -f "${z_target}" && diff -q "${z_tmp}" "${z_target}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    buc_log_args "Context file already up to date: ${z_target}"
  else
    cp "${z_tmp}" "${z_target}" || buc_die "Failed to write: ${z_target}"
    buc_log_args "Generated: ${z_target}"
  fi

  buc_success "Context generation complete"
}

######################################################################
# Furnish and Main

zrblm_furnish() {
  buc_doc_env "BURD_BUK_DIR          " "BUK module directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TOOLS_DIR        " "Project tools root directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TEMP_DIR         " "Temporary directory for this invocation (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env "BURD_TABTARGET_DIR    " "Tabtarget directory (dispatch-provided)"
  buc_doc_env_done || return 0

  source "${BURD_CONFIG_DIR}/rbbc_constants.sh"
  local z_rbk_kit_dir="${BURD_TOOLS_DIR}/${RBBC_kit_subdir}"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbcc_Constants.sh" || buc_die "Failed to source rbcc_Constants.sh"

  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"         || buc_die "Failed to source buym_yelp.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buh_handbook.sh"      || buc_die "Failed to source buh_handbook.sh"
  source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"     || buc_die "Failed to source buz_zipper.sh"
  source "${z_rbk_kit_dir}/rbz_zipper.sh"    || buc_die "Failed to source rbz_zipper.sh"
  zbuz_kindle
  zrbz_kindle
}

buc_execute rblm_ "Lifecycle Marshal" zrblm_furnish "$@"

# eof

return 0
  fi

  test -z "${BURE_COUNTDOWN:-}" || buc_die "BURE_COUNTDOWN must be 'skip' or unset, got '${BURE_COUNTDOWN}'"

  buc_step "Countdown: ${z_seconds}s to cancel (Ctrl-C)"
  sleep 1
  printf '%b ' "${BUC_yellow}${z_prompt}${BUC_reset}" >/dev/tty
  local z_i
  for (( z_i=z_seconds; z_i>=1; z_i-- )); do
    printf '%d... ' "$z_i" >/dev/tty
    sleep 1
  done
  printf '\n' >/dev/tty
}

buc_require() {
  local z_prompt="$1"
  local z_required_value="$2"

  if test "${BURE_CONFIRM:-}" = "skip"; then
    buc_step "Confirm: (skipped — BURE_CONFIRM=skip)"
    return 0
  fi

  test -z "${BURE_CONFIRM:-}" || buc_die "BURE_CONFIRM must be 'skip' or unset, got '${BURE_CONFIRM}'"

  sleep 1
  printf '%b\n' "${BUC_yellow}${z_prompt}${BUC_reset}" >/dev/tty
  printf 'Type %s to confirm: ' "${z_required_value}" >/dev/tty
  local z_input
  read -r z_input </dev/tty
  test "${z_input}" = "${z_required_value}" || buc_die "Confirmation failed — expected '${z_required_value}', got '${z_input}'"
}

  set -e
  local prefix="$1"
  local title="$2"

content.text:
**Failure: transient network timeout in verification tail, not a logic bug.**

Mechanism: `ordain_conjure` cloud build SUCCEEDED (4m0s) and vouch SUCCEEDED (1m30s). The case has a verification tail per BBAA7's absorbed-tail design (wrest+summon+inspect+plumb+rekon+rmi). The first step — `wrest` — timed out on `docker login us-central1-docker.pkg.dev`:

```
Error response from daemon: Get "https://us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/v2/":
... net/http: request canceled while waiting for connection
(Client.Timeout exceeded while awaiting headers)
```

Pattern signal: 4 `curl rc=28` warnings (curl operation timeout) fired during vouch polling 1-2 minutes earlier. Network instability around `*.docker.pkg.dev` at this moment, not code.

**Cost so far this attempt:** 2 GCP projects spent (pristine throwaway + canonical), ~20 min wall-clock to this point.

**Specific repair:** marshal-zero + push + rerun rbw-tP. Per RELEASE.md failure mode contract — no patch-and-continue.

But: re-running immediately may hit the same transient. Want me to retry now, or wait for network to settle? Either path I do; just surfacing the cost-vs-likelihood question since each attempt burns 2 fresh GCP projects.

file.content:
local -r z_pkg_path="${z_locator%:*}"
  local -r z_tag="${z_locator##*:}"
  test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in locator"
  test -n "${z_tag}"      || buc_die "Tag is empty in locator"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" \
    || buc_die "Director credential not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token"

  buc_step "Logging into container registry"
  local -r z_full_ref="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${z_locator}"

  echo "${z_token}" | docker login -u oauth2accesstoken --password-stdin "https://${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}" \
    || buc_die "Container runtime authentication failed"

  buc_step "Pulling image: ${z_full_ref}"
  docker pull "${z_full_ref}" || buc_die "Image pull failed"

  local z_image_id
  docker inspect --format='{{.Id}}' "${z_full_ref}" > "${ZRBFC_SCRATCH_FILE}" 2>/dev/null \
    || buc_die "Failed to get image ID"
  z_image_id=$(<"${ZRBFC_SCRATCH_FILE}")

  echo ""
  echo "Image wrested: ${z_full_ref}"
  echo "Local image ID: ${z_image_id}"

  buc_success "Image wrest complete"
}

rbfl_audit_hallmarks() {
  zrbfl_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Audit hallmarks — list all hallmark identifiers in registry"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" \
    || buc_die "Director credential not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  buc_step "Enumerating hallmarks under ${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/"
  zrbfc_list_packages_capture "${z_token}" "${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}"

  if ! test -s "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"; then
    buc_info "No hallmarks found under ${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/"
    buc_success "Audit complete — 0 hallmarks"
    return 0
  fi

  echo ""
  printf "  %s\n" "HALLMARK"
  printf "  %s\n" "------------------------------"

  local z_count=0

I've performed the web search for your query. The results show multiple discussions about this Docker error across various community forums and GitHub issues.

Here are the key findings:

**Error Description:**
The error "request canceled while waiting for connection (Client.Timeout exceeded while awaiting headers)" occurs when attempting to pull or login to Docker registries.

**Common Root Causes:**

1. **Proxy Configuration:** Most often, this issue is caused by the machine running the Docker command being behind a proxy, which prevents successful communication to Docker registries.

2. **DNS Resolution Issues:** In some cases, the problem was resolved by modifying the `/etc/resolv.conf` file with updated nameserver entries.

The search results primarily discuss this error in the context of connecting to Docker Hub (registry-1.docker.io), though the same timeout mechanism can affect other registries like us-central1-docker.pkg.dev (Google Artifact Registry). The error generally indicates a network connectivity or timeout issue preventing the Docker client from establishing a proper connection to the registry.

The search results provide information about Docker authentication with Google Cloud Artifact Registry and OAuth2 token authentication. Here are the key findings:

**Authentication Methods:**
Docker can be authenticated with Artifact Registry using `gcloud auth print-access-token` with the username `oauth2accesstoken`.

**Common OAuth2 Token Errors:**
Several authentication timeout and failure scenarios are documented:

1. Connection timeout errors can occur when attempting to fetch OAuth tokens, such as "read udp ... i/o timeout" when trying to connect to the token endpoint.

2. Failed OAuth token fetches can occur during Docker buildx operations, with errors like "failed to fetch oauth token: unexpected status: 401 Unauthorized".

3. Docker authentication can fail with errors when reaching the token endpoint, such as unexpected 401 status responses from the authentication server.

**Token Endpoint Configuration:**
Google Artifact Registry includes specific token endpoint configurations in the authentication challenge, with the Bearer realm pointing to a repository-specific token endpoint.

**Transient Issues:**
The search results suggest that token refresh timing may be a factor in some failures, with a suggestion that tokens might be requested on-demand rather than at startup to avoid expiration during long-running operations.

Based on the search results, here are the key findings about `docker login` intermittent timeout issues and best practices:

## Common Causes

Intermittent timeouts when authenticating to a private registry can occur, with errors showing "Client.Timeout exceeded while awaiting headers". Most often, this issue is caused by the machine running the Docker command being behind a proxy, preventing successful communication to hub.docker.com.

## Known Issues

Docker login sometimes times out, and the authentication backend on private docker registries can take longer than the docker daemon's Client.Timeout, though the login request would succeed if the daemon waited longer. There appears to be no way to configure this docker daemon timeout value.

## Retry Behavior

Intermittent errors occur when attempting to log in to Docker on self-hosted runners, but the workflow works correctly if the failed job is re-run, suggesting that retrying the operation can be a viable workaround for transient failures.

## Solutions

The issue may be related to DNS server configuration; switching from Cloudflare DNS (1.0.0.1, 1.1.1.1) to Google DNS (8.8.8.8, 8.8.4.4) resolved docker pull failures.

The search results confirm that `docker login` timeout issues are intermittent and transient in nature, making retry logic a reasonable approach for handling these failures in automation contexts.

content.text:
## Diagnosis

**This is the well-documented docker daemon Client.Timeout transient, not a code bug.**

| Evidence | Implication |
|---|---|
| `curl https://us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/v2/` clean now (134ms, 401 as expected) | Endpoint healthy |
| `gcloud artifacts docker images list` confirms image, attest-amd64, attest-arm64, about, vouch all in GAR with correct tags | Conjure + vouch fully succeeded — failure is purely post-success verification |
| Conjure's docker push (context image to same registry) succeeded ~4 min before the wrest failure | Same daemon, same registry — transient, not configuration |
| 4 curl rc=28 warnings during vouch polling, all recovered (3-consecutive threshold never tripped) | Connectivity flake at this moment, ride-through worked at the build-poll layer |
| `RBRR_DNS_SERVER=8.8.8.8` (Google DNS) | DNS-flake remediation already applied |

**Industry signal (web search):**
- moby/moby#44350: docker daemon's Client.Timeout for the auth backend is non-configurable; auth can legitimately take longer than the timeout, daemon kills it before the registry would have responded successfully
- actions/runner#3384: confirms "intermittent — re-running the failed job works"
- Standard remediation across the corpus: **retry**, because it's transient daemon behavior, not network or auth misconfig

**Structural asymmetry surfaced (not a fix proposal — observation):** `rbfc_FoundryCore.sh` poll loop already tolerates this exact transient class with 3-consecutive-failure tolerance (lines 252-287). The wrest path's `docker login` (`rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh:745-746`) is single-shot, so the same transient class is fatal where it's tolerated 50 lines away.

## Wise-choice frame

Three shapes the operator might want to weigh, named only:

1. **Retry now** — transient is gone (probes clean), industry says retry, costs 2 fresh GCP projects + ~1h. Likelihood-of-success is high but not 100%.
2. **Wait, then retry** — defer to reduce repeat-transient probability. No deterministic signal for "settled," so this is gut-feel.
3. **Slate retry-shape pace before retry** — close the structural asymmetry first (docker-login retry to match build-poll's 3-consecutive tolerance), then retry the gauntlet. Trades one fix-pace's labor against the next attempt's hit-the-same-bug risk.

Per Mid-execution posture I won't recommend among them — pace scope and segmentation are operator territory.

Sources:
- [moby/moby#44350: docker login timeout should be configurable](https://github.com/moby/moby/issues/44350)
- [actions/runner#3384: Intermittent error: Docker Login failed with exit code 1](https://github.com/actions/runner/issues/3384)
- [Docker daemon unable to access registry — Client.Timeout exceeded while awaiting headers (moby#43383)](https://github.com/moby/moby/issues/43383)
- [How to resolve the Docker "Timeout exceeded while awaiting headers" error (TechRepublic)](https://www.techrepublic.com/article/resolve-docker-timeout-headers-error/)
- [Docker pull results in "Request canceled while waiting for connection" (Docker Forums)](https://forums.docker.com/t/docker-pull-results-in-request-canceled-while-waiting-for-connection-client-timeout-exceeded-while-awaiting-headers/73064)

lastPrompt: diagnose, but do not attempt fix yet.

input.content:
# jjezs_slate docker-login-retry-shape

## Character

Design conversation requiring judgment. Mechanism choice must follow operator concurrence — load-bearing slate-time instruction.

## Issue evidence

Failed in BBAAX first-run gauntlet attempt at `rbtdro_onboarding_ordain_conjure` verification tail. Conjure cloud build (4m0s) and vouch (1m30s) both succeeded. All GAR artifacts confirmed via gcloud post-failure (image, attest-amd64, attest-arm64, about, vouch tagged correctly). Failure was wrest's `docker login` against `us-central1-docker.pkg.dev`:

    Error response from daemon: Get "https://us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/v2/":
    net/http: request canceled while waiting for connection
    (Client.Timeout exceeded while awaiting headers)

Endpoint probed clean post-failure (curl 134ms, 401 as expected; gcloud listing fast). Documented docker daemon transient per moby/moby#44350: non-configurable Client.Timeout fires before the registry auth backend responds; industry remediation across the corpus is retry. Same case had 4 curl rc=28 warnings during the preceding vouch poll, absorbed by the existing 3-consecutive tolerance.

## Asymmetry to close

`Tools/rbk/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh` poll loop tolerates this transient class with a 3-consecutive policy (BCG-clean, in production).
`Tools/rbk/rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh` `rbfl_wrest`'s docker login is single-shot, fatal on the same transient class. Same daemon, same registry boundary, different fate.

## Scope

Retry shape for transient-vulnerable docker daemon ↔ registry single-shot calls. Immediate surface is wrest's docker login; design surface should include other single-shot daemon calls crossing the same boundary (login, pull, push, inspect with registry round-trip).

## Discuss before implementing — load-bearing

Mount agent surfaces the design space and proposes a recommended shape; operator concurrence precedes landing. No pre-baking at slate-time. Design questions to seat at the table belong in the conversation, not the docket.

## Out of scope

- Curl/gcloud transient audit beyond the docker daemon path — distinct class
- Build-poll loop changes (already tolerant)
- Generic retry framework — only what the surveyed surface requires

## References

- moby/moby#44350 — non-configurable docker daemon Client.Timeout
- BBAAX inline-fix commits 24e24651 (depot Cloud Build URL) and dd5893ba (Mid-execution posture rule) — first-run iteration arc context

**Second attempt, different transient class** — not docker login this time.

Mechanism:
- `rbgg_invest_retriever` POST'd to IAM, got back `uid: 109130253071978226342` (SA created in write plane)
- Immediately followed with `List service account keys` (preflight: ensure no USER_MANAGED keys)
- 404: `Service account ... does not exist` — IAM read-after-write consistency lag, write-plane create not yet visible on read endpoints

Classic GCP IAM eventual-consistency race. Heat ₣BB has prior pace `BBAAn sa-key-propagation-probe` (per swim lanes) which modified `rbgg_Governor.sh` — there's already a probe pattern in play. Need to check whether this code path uses it.

file.content:
local z_display_name="$2"
  local z_description="$3"
  local z_role="$4"

  local z_account_email="${z_account_name}@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL}"

  buc_step 'Get OAuth token from admin'
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"

  buc_step "Create request JSON for ${z_account_name}"
  jq -n                                      \
    --arg account_id "${z_account_name}"     \
    --arg display_name "${z_display_name}"   \
    --arg description "${z_description}"     \
    '{
      accountId: $account_id,
      serviceAccount: {
        displayName: $display_name,
        description: $description
      }
    }' > "${ZRBGG_PREFIX}create_request.json" || buc_die "Failed to create request JSON"

  buc_step 'Create service account via REST API'
  rbgu_http_json "POST" "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}" "${z_token}" \
    "${ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE}" "${ZRBGG_PREFIX}create_request.json"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "Create service account" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE}"

  local z_sa_uid
  z_sa_uid=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_CREATE}" '.uniqueId') \
    || buc_die "Failed to get uniqueId from SA creation response"
  buc_info "Service account created: ${z_account_email} (uid: ${z_sa_uid})"

  rbgu_poll_until_ok "SA propagation (by email)" \
    "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}/${z_account_email}" "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_VERIFY}"

  buc_step 'Preflight: ensure no existing USER_MANAGED keys (manual cleanup path)'

  buc_log_args 'List keys'
  rbgu_http_json "GET"                                                        \
    "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}/${z_account_email}${RBGC_PATH_KEYS}"        \
                                      "${z_token}" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_KEYS}"
  rbgu_http_require_ok "List service account keys" "${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_KEYS}"

  buc_log_args 'Count existing user-managed keys'
  local z_user_keys
  z_user_keys=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "${ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_KEYS}" \
                 '[.keys[]? | select(.keyType=="USER_MANAGED")] | length') \
    || buc_die "Failed to parse service account keys"

  if test "${z_user_keys}" -gt 0; then
    buc_log_args 'Provide a console URL to delete keys manually, then rerun this command'
    local z_sa_email_enc="${z_account_email//@/%40}"
    local z_keys_url="${RBGC_CONSOLE_URL}iam-admin/serviceaccounts/details/${z_sa_email_enc}?project=${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}"

    buc_warn "Found ${z_user_keys} existing USER_MANAGED key(s) on ${z_account_email}."
    buc_info "Open Console, select the **Keys** tab, delete old keys, then rerun:"
    buc_info "  ${z_keys_url}"
    buc_die  "Aborting to avoid minting additional keys."
  fi

  buc_step 'Generate service account key (with propagation retry)'
  local z_key_req="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgg_key_request.json"
  printf '%s' '{"privateKeyType": "TYPE_GOOGLE_CREDENTIALS_FILE"}' > "${z_key_req}"

  local z_key_attempt=0
  local z_key_infix=""
  local z_key_code=""
  while :; do
    z_key_attempt=$((z_key_attempt + 1))
    z_key_infix="${ZRBGG_INFIX_KEY}-attempt${z_key_attempt}"
    rbgu_http_json "POST" "${RBGD_API_SERVICE_ACCOUNTS}/${z_account_email}${RBGC_PATH_KEYS}" \
                                           "${z_token}" "${z_key_infix}" "${z_key_req}"

    z_key_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_key_infix}") || z_key_code=""

    if test "${z_key_code}" = "200"; then
      break
    fi

    if test "${z_key_code}" = "404" && test "${z_key_attempt}" -lt "${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX}"; then
      buc_warn "keys.create returned 404 (SA write-path propagation delay), retry ${z_key_attempt}/${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX} in ${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC}s..."
      sleep "${RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC}"
      continue
    fi

content.text:
## Diagnosis

This is **not** a new bug class — it's the **same eventual-consistency class already mitigated two steps below the failure**.

`rbgg_Governor.sh` flow at SA creation:

| Line | Call | Tolerance shape |
|---|---|---|
| 193-194 | `GET /serviceAccounts/<email>` (poll-until-OK propagation probe) | ✓ existing |
| **196-202** | **`GET /serviceAccounts/<email>/keys` (LIST keys preflight) — single-shot** | **✗ FAILS HERE** |
| 240-243 | `POST /serviceAccounts/<email>/keys` (keys.create) — retry-on-404 with `RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX` budget | ✓ existing |

The keys subresource has propagation lag relative to the SA itself — already known and mitigated for keys.create just below, but the LIST-keys preflight one step earlier slipped through. Two passes through the same propagation race in 50 lines: the first has retry, the middle (which we just hit) is single-shot.

## Specific repair

Apply the existing retry-on-404 pattern from lines 240-243 (keys.create) to the LIST-keys preflight at lines 199-202. Same primitive, same retry budget, structurally identical change. Lifts the asymmetry without inventing a new mechanism.

## Segmentation question (operator territory)

Per BBAAX docket criteria, a one-pattern-applied-to-parallel-call-site sits in the "tiny inline" lane: "fix and re-run within this pace." But this is the second different transient in two attempts, which suggests a pattern question worth pausing on:

- Inline this fix in BBAAX, retry, see what surfaces next
- Or pause BBAAX and let ₢BBAA8 broaden scope from "docker-login retry shape" to "transient-tolerance audit across single-shot daemon/IAM paths"

I'll do whichever you say. Both are visible from here; you decide segmentation.

568	rbgu_poll_until_ok() {
569	  zrbgu_sentinel
570	
571	  local -r z_label="${1}"
572	  local -r z_url="${2}"
573	  local -r z_token="${3}"
574	  local -r z_infix="${4}"
575	
576	  local z_elapsed=0
577	  while :; do
578	    local z_poll_infix="${z_infix}-${z_elapsed}s"
579	    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_poll_infix}" || true
580	
581	    local z_code
582	    z_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_poll_infix}") || z_code=""
583	
584	    if test "${z_code}" = "200"; then
585	      buc_log_args "${z_label} ready after ${z_elapsed} seconds"
586	      return 0
587	    fi
588	
589	    test "${z_elapsed}" -ge "${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}" && buc_die "${z_label}: timeout after ${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}s"
590	    buc_log_args "${z_label} not ready (HTTP ${z_code}), waiting ${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}s..."
591	    sleep "${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}"
592	    z_elapsed=$((z_elapsed + RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC))
593	  done
594	}
595	
596	# Predicate: Check if resource was newly created and apply propagation delay
597	rbgu_newly_created_delay() {
598	  zrbgu_sentinel
599	
600	  local -r z_infix="${1}"
601	  local -r z_resource="${2}"

file.content:
rbgu_poll_until_ok() {
  zrbgu_sentinel

  local -r z_label="${1}"
  local -r z_url="${2}"
  local -r z_token="${3}"
  local -r z_infix="${4}"

  local z_elapsed=0
  while :; do
    local z_poll_infix="${z_infix}-${z_elapsed}s"
    rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_poll_infix}" || true

    local z_code
    z_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_poll_infix}") || z_code=""

    if test "${z_code}" = "200"; then
      buc_log_args "${z_label} ready after ${z_elapsed} seconds"
      return 0
    fi

    test "${z_elapsed}" -ge "${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}" && buc_die "${z_label}: timeout after ${RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC}s"
    buc_log_args "${z_label} not ready (HTTP ${z_code}), waiting ${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}s..."
    sleep "${RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC}"
    z_elapsed=$((z_elapsed + RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC))
  done
}

# Predicate: Check if resource was newly created and apply propagation delay
rbgu_newly_created_delay() {
  zrbgu_sentinel

  local -r z_infix="${1}"
  local -r z_resource="${2}"
  local -r z_delay="${3}"

  local z_code
  z_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "${z_infix}") || return 1

  if test "${z_code}" = "200" || test "${z_code}" = "201"; then
    buc_step "Resource ${z_resource} newly created, waiting ${z_delay}s for propagation"
    sleep "${z_delay}"
  fi
}

rbgu_extract_json_to_rbra() {
  zrbgu_sentinel

  local -r z_json_path="$1"
  local -r z_rbra_path="$2"
  local -r z_lifetime_sec="$3"
  local -r z_expected_project_id="${4:-}"

  test -f "${z_json_path}" || buc_die "Service account JSON not found: ${z_json_path}"

  buc_info "Extracting service account credentials from JSON"

  buc_log_args 'Extract fields'
  local z_client_email
  z_client_email=$(jq -r '.client_email' "${z_json_path}") \
                                        || buc_die "Failed to extract client_email"
  test -n "${z_client_email}"           || buc_die "Empty client_email in JSON"
  test    "${z_client_email}" != "null" || buc_die "Null client_email in JSON"

  local z_private_key
  z_private_key=$(jq -r '.private_key' "${z_json_path}") \
                                       || buc_die "Failed to extract private_key"
  test -n "${z_private_key}"           || buc_die "Empty private_key in JSON"
  test    "${z_private_key}" != "null" || buc_die "Null private_key in JSON"

lastPrompt: Might we get a better result by incorporating a currently available helper that handles transients better?  Or do we need to improve current with a more bespoke amendment?

content.text:
**Yes — `rbgu_poll_until_ok` is the right helper.** Already used two lines above the failure for the parallel SA-by-email propagation probe.

`rbgu_Utility.sh:568` — polls a GET URL until HTTP 200, with `RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC` budget and `RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC` interval. Exactly the eventual-consistency primitive this case needs.

| Call site | Pattern |
|---|---|
| Line 193-194: GET-by-email propagation probe | uses `rbgu_poll_until_ok` ✓ |
| **Line 196-202: LIST-keys preflight** | bare `rbgu_http_json` + `rbgu_http_require_ok` — **what just failed** |
| Line 228-243: POST keys.create | bespoke retry-on-404 inline loop — necessarily bespoke (writes can't safely poll-until-OK; idempotency would mint N keys) |

**Why the bespoke retry-on-404 exists at keys.create:** it's a write. The helper handles GET propagation — that's its contract. Writes need bespoke handling because each retry has side effects.

**Why the LIST-keys preflight is wrong shape:** it's a GET, no side effect, propagation-vulnerable. Using the helper is the obviously-correct pattern; bare-GET-then-require-OK was the gap.

**One subtlety on the integration:** `rbgu_poll_until_ok` writes per-iteration response files (`z_infix-<N>s`), not the canonical infix. The current downstream code at lines 206-207 reads `ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_KEYS` to count USER_MANAGED keys. So the fix shape is one of:

- **Two-call:** `rbgu_poll_until_ok` to wait, then a separate `rbgu_http_json` to capture the canonical response for parsing. Simple, no helper change.
- **Helper enhancement:** add a "capture final response to canonical infix" parameter to `rbgu_poll_until_ok`. Cleaner in the long run, scope creep here.

Two-call is what BBAAX's "tiny inline" lane is for. Helper enhancement is what BBAA8's design conversation is for (it'd also benefit future propagation-tolerant reads — same pattern).

Want me to do the two-call inline fix here in BBAAX, and let BBAA8 consider absorbing the helper enhancement into its scope?

rbgg_invest_retriever() {
  zrbgg_sentinel

  local z_identity="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "Invest a Retriever service account for an identity"
  buc_doc_param "identity" "Identity (required) — composes ${RBCC_role_retriever}-<identity>"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_identity}" || buc_die "Identity required"

  local z_account_name="${RBCC_role_retriever}-${z_identity}"
  local z_account_email="${z_account_name}@${RBGD_SA_EMAIL_FULL}"

  buc_step "Investing Retriever service account: ${z_account_name}"

  zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key                                          \
    "${z_account_name}"                                                        \
    "Recipe Bottle Retriever (${z_identity})"                                  \
    "Read-only access to Google Artifact Registry - identity: ${z_identity}"   \
    "${RBCC_role_retriever}" > /dev/null || buc_die "Failed to create Retriever SA"

  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgu_get_governor_token_capture) || buc_die "Failed to get admin token"

  buc_step 'Adding Artifact Registry Reader role'
  rbgi_add_project_iam_role                 \
    "${z_token}"                            \
    "Grant Artifact Registry Reader"        \
    "${RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE}"              \
    "${RBGC_ROLE_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_READER}"  \
    "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}"     \
    "retriever-reader"

  buc_step 'Adding Container Analysis Occurrences Viewer role'
  rbgi_add_project_iam_role                              \
    "${z_token}"                                         \
    "Grant Container Analysis Occurrences Viewer"        \
    "${RBGD_PROJECT_RESOURCE}"                           \
    "${RBGC_ROLE_CONTAINERANALYSIS_OCCURRENCES_VIEWER}"  \
    "serviceAccount:${z_account_email}"                  \
    "retriever-analysis"

  buc_info "RBRA file written: ${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE}"
  buc_info ""
  buc_info "Move the RBRA file to a safe place — typically delivered to the"
  buc_info "operator who consumes this identity. When the invester is also the"
  buc_info "consumer, the local role slot is the canonical destination:"
  buc_bare "        mv ${RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE} ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
}

params.intent: Insert rbgu_poll_until_ok probe before LIST-keys preflight in zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key. Second BBAAX gauntlet attempt failed at rbtdrk_retriever_invest with HTTP 404 'Service account does not exist' on the LIST keys preflight (rbgg_Governor.sh:196-202), immediately after a successful SA create returning a uid. The post-create rbgu_poll_until_ok at line 193-194 confirmed the SA was visible at the by-email GET endpoint, but the keys subresource has its own eventual-consistency lag relative to the SA itself — already known and mitigated for keys.create at line 240-243 with retry-on-404, but the LIST-keys preflight one step earlier was single-shot. Fix is the two-call shape: rbgu_poll_until_ok against the keys subpath URL absorbs the propagation delay, then the existing rbgu_http_json + rbgu_http_require_ok pair captures the canonical response into ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_KEYS for downstream USER_MANAGED counting at line 209-210. New ZRBGG_INFIX_KEYS_PROBE='keys_probe' constant added next to ZRBGG_INFIX_LIST_KEYS to avoid temp-file basename collision with ZRBGG_INFIX_VERIFY (which the by-email probe at line 194 consumes). Single-point-of-fix covers both invest paths — rbgg_invest_retriever and rbgg_invest_director both route through zrbgg_create_service_account_with_key. Operator question 'use existing helper or bespoke amendment' answered: rbgu_poll_until_ok is the right helper, already used parallel-shape at line 194 for the SA-by-email probe; bespoke retry-on-404 at keys.create is necessarily inline because writes can't safely poll-until-OK (idempotency would mint N keys on retry), but reads are exactly the helper's contract. Asymmetry observed: rbfc_FoundryCore.sh poll loop tolerates curl rc=28 timeouts with 3-consecutive policy, rbgu_poll_until_ok tolerates GET propagation 404s, but rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh wrest's docker login is single-shot — the broader transient-tolerance survey is slated as ₢BBAA8 docker-login-retry-shape for design conversation per operator's pre-implementation-discussion constraint. This commit is a tactical inline application of the existing helper to close one specific call-site asymmetry, not a structural change. shellcheck -S error clean. Validation deferred to clean-chat BBAAX retry per operator instruction.
